pageCaption.value: From its earliest days, Rolex has pursued perpetual excellence and, as part of this, a commitment to a Perpetual Planet.

metadescription.value: Discover the stories of those devising solutions for a better future, supported by Rolex through the Perpetual Planet Initiative. Watch the video on rolex.org.

title.value: The Rolex Perpetual Planet Initiative: Solutions of a better future

text.value: From its earliest days, Rolex has pursued perpetual excellence, which applies to all its endeavours including a commitment to a Perpetual Planet over many decades. The company supports those devising solutions that will restore balance to our ecosystems as part of its Perpetual Planet Initiative. All of the individuals and organizations highlighted in this film, or those associated with the brand through long-standing partnerships, have stories that draw attention to the planet’s fragility and demonstrate how they are helping to protect the environment. As oceanographer Sylvia Earle says: “It is our obligation to save our planet for future generations.”

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Malpelo Island Hope Spot - Episode 2

pageCaption.value: Naturalist and professional diver Sandra Bessudo has worked for decades to conserve marine life around Malpelo Island, off the coast of Colombia.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Malpelo Island Hope Spot - Episode 2

metadescription.value: As the Malpelo Island Hope Spot Champion, diver Sandra Bessudo leads expeditions to monitor the region twice a year. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Mission Blue: Malpelo Island Hope Spot - Episode 2

pageCaption.value: Hunters and fishermen almost eradicated Atlantic puffins from Maine’s offshore islands in the 19th century. The innovative techniques that 1987 Rolex Laureate Stephen W. Kress used to bring them back have inspired major changes in seabird conservation.

metadescription.value: Hunters and fishermen almost eradicated Atlantic puffins from Maine’s offshore islands in the 19th century. The innovative techniques that 1987 Rolex Laureate Stephen W. Kress used to bring them back have inspired major changes in seabird conservation.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards for Enterprise - Latest Laureates - Video

pageCaption.value: As part of its Perpetual Planet Initiative, Rolex is pleased to announce the new Laureates of the Rolex Awards for Enterprise.

metadescription.value: Discover the 2023 Rolex Awards Laureates, five pioneers striving towards a perpetual planet through ambitious projects. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: The 10-year anniversary of the Rolex Deepsea Challenge - Video

pageCaption.value: Ten years ago, on 26 March 2012, Rolex Testimonee James Cameron made history as the first person to dive solo to the deepest part of the Earth, the Pacific Ocean’s Mariana Trench, at 10,908 metres (35,787 feet).

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex Deepsea Challenge - A decade on with James Cameron - Video

metadescription.value: On the 10-year anniversary of his expedition, Rolex Testimonee James Cameron reflects on his journey to the deep sea. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Rolex Deepsea Challenge - A decade on with James Cameron - Video

pageTitle.value: Ice towers in the desert - Video

pageCaption.value: Sonam Wangchuk lives and works in the Ladakh region of India, high in the Himalayas. This harsh mountain area is experiencing acute water shortages due to climate change. Sonam, who is an engineer and educator, found a simple but ingenious way to store water using artificial glaciers that will irrigate trees and crops.
